Appendix, Section 5.1 (page 20).

It remains open to prove such bounds in the range \((\lambda_-(\alpha), \lambda_+(\alpha))\); and once proved, the result will also imply the almost sure convergence of \(N_t/t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\) as \(t \to \infty\).
title: Large-deviation bounds for stake-volume Markov chain in intermediate deviation regime
background_latex:
Consider the discrete-time, nondecreasing Markov chain \((N_t)_{t\ge 0}\) on \(\{N,N+1,\dots\}\) defined by \(N_0=N\in\mathbb{N}_+\) and the one-step transition
\[
N_{t+1}=\begin{cases}
N_t+1,&\text{with probability }N_t^{-\alpha},\\
N_t,&\text{with probability }1-N_t^{-\alpha},
\end{cases}
\qquad t\ge 0,
\]
where \(\alpha>0\) is fixed. (Equivalently, the increment \(\Delta_t:=N_{t+1}-N_t\in\{0,1\}\) is Bernoulli with parameter \(N_t^{-\alpha}\) conditional on \(N_t\).)

A deterministic growth scale for \(N_t\) is \(t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\), and the paper studies tail probabilities of the form \(\mathbb{P}(N_t\le \lambda t^{1/(1+\alpha)})\) and \(\mathbb{P}(N_t\ge \lambda t^{1/(1+\alpha)})\) as \(t\to\infty\). It introduces the rate function
\[
f_\alpha(\lambda)=(1+\alpha)\lambda\log\lambda-(1+\alpha)\lambda+\lambda^{-\alpha},\qquad \lambda>0.
\]
This function has two positive roots \(\lambda_-(\alpha)<\lambda_+(\alpha)\) and satisfies \(f_\alpha(\lambda)>0\) for \(\lambda\in(0,\lambda_-(\alpha))\cup(\lambda_+(\alpha),\infty)\). The paper proves exponential tail bounds with exponent proportional to \(t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\) in those two outer regimes.

The chain also has a natural “threshold” constant \((1+\alpha)^{1/(1+\alpha)}\) coming from the deterministic fluid-limit scaling \(N_t\approx ((1+\alpha)t)^{1/(1+\alpha)}\), so the intermediate regime \(\lambda\in(\lambda_-(\alpha),\lambda_+(\alpha))\) contains this threshold.
problem_definition_latex:
Prove large-deviation-type exponential bounds for \(N_t\) on the intermediate scale \(t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\) for deviation parameters \(\lambda\in(\lambda_-(\alpha),\lambda_+(\alpha))\). Concretely, establish upper bounds of the form
\[
\mathbb{P}\bigl(N_t\le \lambda t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\bigr)\le \exp\bigl(-c_-(\lambda)\,t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\bigr),\qquad
\mathbb{P}\bigl(N_t\ge \lambda t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\bigr)\le \exp\bigl(-c_+(\lambda)\,t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\bigr)
\]
for appropriate strictly positive rate functions \(c_-(\lambda),c_+(\lambda)\) throughout \(\lambda\in(\lambda_-(\alpha),\lambda_+(\alpha))\), and in particular up to (or “right off”) the threshold \((1+\alpha)^{1/(1+\alpha)}\).
latex:

\qquad t\ge 0,

where \(\alpha>0\) is fixed. (Equivalently, the increment \(\Delta_t:=N_{t+1}-N_t\in\{0,1\}\) is Bernoulli with parameter \(N_t^{-\alpha}\) conditional on \(N_t\).)

A deterministic growth scale for \(N_t\) is \(t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\), and the paper studies tail probabilities of the form \(\mathbb{P}(N_t\le \lambda t^{1/(1+\alpha)})\) and \(\mathbb{P}(N_t\ge \lambda t^{1/(1+\alpha)})\) as \(t\to\infty\). It introduces the rate function

Prove large-deviation-type exponential bounds for \(N_t\) on the intermediate scale \(t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\) for deviation parameters \(\lambda\in(\lambda_-(\alpha),\lambda_+(\alpha))\). Concretely, establish upper bounds of the form
\[
\mathbb{P}\bigl(N_t\le \lambda t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\bigr)\le \exp\bigl(-c_-(\lambda)\,t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\bigr),\qquad
\mathbb{P}\bigl(N_t\ge \lambda t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\bigr)\le \exp\bigl(-c_+(\lambda)\,t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\bigr)
\]
for appropriate strictly positive rate functions \(c_-(\lambda),c_+(\lambda)\) throughout \(\lambda\in(\lambda_-(\alpha),\lambda_+(\alpha))\), and in particular up to (or “right off”) the threshold \((1+\alpha)^{1/(1+\alpha)}\).
open_problem_quote: It remains open to prove such bounds in the range \((\lambda_-(\alpha), \lambda_+(\alpha))\); and once proved, the result will also imply the almost sure convergence of \(N_t/t^{1/(1+\alpha)}\) as \(t \to \infty\).
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The task is to prove exponential tail bounds uniformly over an interval of \(\lambda\), not to disprove a conjectured inequality on a finite instance set. While simulations might suggest correct rate functions, there is no finite counterexample search that resolves the statement.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: An evolutionary coding loop could search for sharper exponential-moment/supermartingale constructions or improved combinatorial decompositions that empirically tighten bounds for \(\mathbb{P}(N_t\approx \lambda t^{1/(1+\alpha)})\) across \(\lambda\). The fitness signal (numerically estimated log-tail probabilities) is computable, but proving the resulting bounds would still require nontrivial analytic justification.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
